While the Trump administration has sought to characterise Cole Tomas Allen, 31, as “anti-Christian”, his college classmates remember him as a “mellow” believer, former Intercept reporter Ken Klippenstein has revealed. On Sunday, Trump told […]

items.title: 1ª Feijoada de Ogum do MST em SP reuniu agroecologia, ancestralidade e solidariedade
items.summary: Cerca de 2 mil pessoas celebraram a divindade africana, que se tornou uma das mais populares nas tradições da religiosidade e cultura negra no Brasil O post 1ª Feijoada de Ogum do MST em SP reuniu agroecologia, ancestralidade e solidariedade apareceu primeiro em MST .

items.title: The treasures of ODI Art Centre
items.summary: A unique museum on the banks of the Chilika lake preserves endangered folk and tribal art and artefacts from Odisha and beyond.

items.title: Notes from Noida
items.summary: When an authoritarian state apparatus that favours factory owners and discourages trade union activity leaves little space for the routine negotiation of grievances, spontaneous “revolt” becomes the vent for accumulated worker anger.

items.title: How Odisha’s 2024 poll data hollows out the Republic
items.summary: Vote tallies from the Assembly and Lok Sabha elections do not match, and the Election Commission’s silence puts electoral democracy itself at risk.

items.title: Himanta Sarma’s hate speech is more divisive than the UGC regulation. Will the Supreme Court stop him?
items.summary: The court ordered suo motu FIRs against hate preachers. As the Assam Chief Minister relentlessly demonises “Miyas”, will the justices apply their own ruling before his venom divides society further?

items.title: What connects floods, landslides, and minority bashing in Uttarakhand?
items.summary: The Char Dham highway project exemplifies how the BJP government’s disregard for environmental regulations and its politics of religious polarisation are two sides of the same authoritarian coin—one that degrades both nature and society.

items.title: India’s Supreme Court could learn one thing from SCOTUS
items.summary: Timing. America’s top court struck down Trump’s tariffs before they stuck. Ours waits until laws have already won. Can that change?

items.title: Surendra Gadling and the justice that must be seen to be denied
items.summary: The human rights lawyer is the only one of the Bhima Koregaon-16 still in jail. Seven years on, charges have not even been framed against him in a case built on a surrendered Maoist’s statement.

items.title: The Fight Against Labour Comes to Starmer’s Back Garden
items.summary: One of the most revealing moments of the ‘loveless landslide’ that brought Labour into power in July 2024 was the count at Keir Starmer’s constituency in central London. At 3am, it was announced that Starmer had, as expected, retained Holborn & St Pancras – but his vote had been cut in half since 2019, largely […]

items.summary: “It all comes down to those four,” said an advocate, “and if they are going to continue to try to hand Trump warrantless surveillance.” The post Meet the Four Democrats Who’ll Decide If Trump Gets His Domestic Spying Law appeared first on The Intercept .

items.title: It Used to Really Matter When People Fired Guns at Presidents
items.summary: It’s an odd thing to wake up on a Monday after a weekend in which someone tried to assassinate the president of the USA, and feel absolutely nothing about it. As far as I know, the Washington Hilton doesn’t specialise in mogadon-laced mojitos. So, odder still to spend your Sunday reading reports of the gunfire […]

items.title: Blood in Barcelona as Balkan Crime Clans Kill Each Other in the Streets
items.summary: With four targeted attacks in Barcelona since July, the city has become the latest battleground in a war between Balkan crime clans that has left a trail of blood across Europe.
